title: A misunderstanding that turned into friendship
summary: Describe a situation where a misunderstanding, embarrassing moment, or awkward first impression eventually became the foundation of a genuine friendship.
topic: A misunderstanding that turned into friendship
prompt: Write 3 paragraphs (350–500 words) describing a misunderstanding — with a classmate, colleague, neighbour, or stranger — that initially created distance or awkwardness but eventually led to a genuine connection. Describe what happened, how it was resolved, and what the friendship has been like since. Use present perfect, past perfect, modal verbs, and at least one 'although' or 'however'.
hintStarters: If I had known at the time what that confusion would lead to…
The misunderstanding was small enough to ignore, but we didn't, and that made…
Although the beginning was awkward enough to make me want to avoid her entirely…

hintVocab.meaningVi: phá vỡ sự ngại ngùng ban đầu
giải thích rõ ràng một hiểu lầm
cười trừ, bỏ qua bằng cách cười
nhìn thấy khía cạnh buồn cười
làm ai đó cảm thấy thoải mái
ở mức độ bề mặt, hời hợt
dần dần được ai đó yêu thích hơn

sampleText:
The first time I met Dilnoza was in the shared kitchen of a student dormitory in Ankara. I had been living there for two weeks and had seen her only from a distance. One evening I took what I thought was my container of leftover rice from the fridge and ate it in my room. Half an hour later she knocked on my door, politely but firmly, holding the empty container that belonged to her. I had not noticed the name written on the lid in small letters. The conversation that followed was brief and considerably more uncomfortable than I would have liked.

Although I apologised immediately, I had expected the matter to end there, with a formal and cool distance maintained between us for the rest of the year. Instead, the following morning she knocked again, this time holding a plate of food she had cooked — enough for two — and explained that she had cooked too much and would I like some. It was such a generous gesture that I could not tell whether it was politeness or a genuine offer, but I accepted. We ate in the corridor because both our rooms were too small, and somewhere in that meal we discovered that we had both moved from different cities, both found the university canteen uninspiring, and both preferred cooking late at night when the kitchen was quiet. The misunderstanding had, accidentally, given us our first real conversation.

That was three years ago. Dilnoza and I have since graduated and moved to different countries — she is now working in Tashkent, I am in Hanoi — but we have kept in touch in the way that only people who have shared a specific, slightly ridiculous experience can. The story of the rice has become a piece of our friendship's foundation. It gets slightly more dramatic each time one of us tells it. What I have come to think is that first impressions are often surface-level readings of a person, and a misunderstanding, for all the awkwardness it creates, can sometimes cut straight through that surface to something more honest.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: I had expected the matter to end there
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: tôi đã kỳ vọng vấn đề sẽ kết thúc ở đó
khoảng cách chính thức và lạnh lùng
một cử chỉ hào phóng đến vậy

sampleAnnotations.phrase: whether it was politeness or a genuine offer
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: liệu đó là phép lịch sự hay lời mời chân thành
thấy căng tin trường đại học không hấp dẫn

sampleAnnotations.phrase: given us our first real conversation
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cho chúng tôi cuộc trò chuyện thực sự đầu tiên
kể từ đó đã tốt nghiệp

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a piece of our friendship's foundation
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một phần nền tảng của tình bạn chúng tôi
kịch tính hơn một chút mỗi lần
những nhận xét hời hợt về một người
cắt thẳng qua bề mặt đó

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past perfect continuous for an ongoing past background
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'I had been living there for two weeks' dùng quá khứ hoàn thành tiếp diễn để mô tả một hành động đang diễn ra liên tục trong quá khứ, kéo dài đến một thời điểm cụ thể khác trong câu chuyện. 'Had been living' nhấn mạnh tính liên tục và khoảng thời gian.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'I had been living there for two weeks' uses the past perfect continuous to describe an ongoing action in the past that had been happening up to a specific point in the story. 'Had been living' emphasises continuity and duration.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present perfect with 'since' for continuing states
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'We have since graduated and moved to different countries' — 'have … graduated' dùng hiện tại hoàn thành để nối sự kiện quá khứ (tốt nghiệp) với hiện tại. 'Since' ở đây không phải là giới từ mà là trạng từ ('since then'), nhấn mạnh sự thay đổi diễn ra kể từ thời điểm được nhắc đến.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'We have since graduated and moved to different countries' — 'have … graduated' uses present perfect to connect a past event (graduating) to the present. Here 'since' is an adverb meaning 'since then', emphasising a change that occurred after the moment described.

sampleTranslationVi:
Lần đầu tiên tôi gặp Dilnoza là ở bếp chung của ký túc xá sinh viên ở Ankara. Tôi đã sống ở đó được hai tuần và chỉ nhìn thấy cô ấy từ xa. Một buổi tối tôi lấy những gì tôi nghĩ là hộp cơm thừa của mình từ tủ lạnh và ăn trong phòng. Nửa tiếng sau cô ấy gõ cửa phòng tôi, lịch sự nhưng kiên quyết, tay cầm chiếc hộp rỗng thuộc về cô ấy. Tôi đã không để ý tên viết trên nắp bằng chữ nhỏ. Cuộc trò chuyện tiếp theo ngắn ngủi và khó chịu hơn đáng kể so với những gì tôi mong muốn.

Mặc dù tôi đã xin lỗi ngay lập tức, tôi đã kỳ vọng vấn đề sẽ kết thúc ở đó, với khoảng cách chính thức và lạnh lùng được duy trì giữa chúng tôi trong suốt năm còn lại. Thay vào đó, sáng hôm sau cô ấy lại gõ cửa, lần này tay cầm đĩa thức ăn cô ấy đã nấu — đủ cho hai người — và giải thích rằng cô ấy đã nấu quá nhiều và tôi có muốn ăn một ít không. Đó là một cử chỉ hào phóng đến mức tôi không thể biết đó là phép lịch sự hay lời mời chân thành, nhưng tôi đã chấp nhận. Chúng tôi ăn ở hành lang vì cả hai phòng đều quá nhỏ, và trong bữa ăn đó chúng tôi khám phá ra rằng cả hai đều chuyển từ những thành phố khác nhau, cả hai đều thấy căng tin trường đại học không hấp dẫn, và cả hai đều thích nấu ăn vào đêm khuya khi bếp vắng vẻ. Sự hiểu lầm đó, một cách tình cờ, đã cho chúng tôi cuộc trò chuyện thực sự đầu tiên.

Đó là ba năm trước. Dilnoza và tôi kể từ đó đã tốt nghiệp và chuyển đến các quốc gia khác nhau — cô ấy hiện đang làm việc ở Tashkent, còn tôi ở Hà Nội — nhưng chúng tôi đã giữ liên lạc theo cách mà chỉ những người đã chia sẻ một trải nghiệm cụ thể, hơi buồn cười mới có thể làm. Câu chuyện về hộp cơm đã trở thành một phần nền tảng của tình bạn chúng tôi. Nó trở nên kịch tính hơn một chút mỗi lần một trong chúng tôi kể. Điều tôi đã nhận ra là những ấn tượng đầu tiên thường là những nhận xét hời hợt về một người, và một sự hiểu lầm, với tất cả sự lúng túng nó tạo ra, đôi khi có thể cắt thẳng qua bề mặt đó đến điều gì đó thật thà hơn.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the past perfect continuous to show an ongoing background state?
options: I had been living there for two weeks.
I have been living there for two weeks.
I had lived there for two weeks.
I was living there since two weeks.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past perfect continuous (had been + -ing) describes an ongoing background action at a point in the past narrative. 'For two weeks' indicates duration.
mcQuestions.hint: Ongoing past background + duration: had been + -ing + for.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses present perfect with the adverb 'since'?
options: We have since graduated and moved to different countries.
We have since graduate and moved to different countries.
We since graduated and moved to different countries.
We had since graduated and moved to different countries.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present perfect: 'have + past participle'; 'since' as an adverb means 'since that time'.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Have since + past participle' — 'since' as adverb meaning 'since then'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'although' correctly to set up a contrast?
options: Although I apologised immediately, I had expected the matter to end there.
Although I apologised immediately, but I had expected the matter to end there.
I apologised immediately, although I had expected the matter to end there, however.
Although I apologised immediately so I had expected the matter to end there.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Although + clause, main clause' — no additional 'but' or 'however' in the same sentence.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Although' clause + comma + main clause — do not add 'but' after the comma.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'such a + adjective + noun' for emphasis?
options: It was such a generous gesture that I could not tell.
It was so a generous gesture that I could not tell.
It was such generous gesture that I could not tell.
It was such a generous gesture that I could not told.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Such a + adjective + noun + that' is the correct structure for emphasis; 'could not tell' uses the bare infinitive after modal.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Such a + adjective + noun + that' — not 'so a'; 'could not + bare infinitive'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option correctly uses 'whether' to introduce an indirect question?
options: I could not tell whether it was politeness or a genuine offer.
I could not tell that it was politeness or a genuine offer.
I could not tell if it was politeness or genuine offer.
I could not tell whether was it politeness or a genuine offer.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Whether … or' is used to present two alternatives in an indirect question; word order is subject + verb after 'whether'.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Whether + subject + verb … or + alternative' — normal word order after 'whether'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses past perfect to show an expectation that existed before a main past event?
options: I had expected the matter to end there.
I expected the matter to have ended there.
I have expected the matter to end there.
I was expecting the matter had ended there.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Had expected' (past perfect) shows the expectation existed before the narrative events that followed.
mcQuestions.hint: Expectation before the main story events: had + expected.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'what + clause' as a subject?
options: What I have come to think is that first impressions are surface-level.
What I have come to think that first impressions are surface-level.
Which I have come to think is that first impressions are surface-level.
What I have come to think is first impressions are surface-level.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'What + subject + verb + is + that + clause' is the correct cleft sentence structure; 'is that' bridges the two clauses.
mcQuestions.hint: Cleft: What + clause + is + that + clause — 'is that' is required.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase correctly uses a relative clause to identify a group?
options: People who have shared a specific experience can stay close.
People which have shared a specific experience can stay close.
People whom have shared a specific experience can stay close.
People, who have shared a specific experience, can stay close.
mcQuestions.explanation: Defining relative clause: 'who' for people, no commas, present perfect 'have shared'.
mcQuestions.hint: Defining relative clause for people: who + verb, no commas.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option correctly uses 'keep in touch' in the present perfect?
options: We have kept in touch.
We have kept in the touch.
We have keeping in touch.
We kept in touch since then.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Keep in touch' is a fixed phrase; present perfect 'have kept' without an article ('in touch', not 'in the touch').
mcQuestions.hint: Fixed phrase: 'keep in touch' (no article); present perfect: have kept.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly compares using 'more … than'?
options: The conversation was considerably more uncomfortable than I would have liked.
The conversation was considerably more uncomfortable as I would have liked.
The conversation was considerably more uncomfortable than I would liked.
The conversation was considerably more uncomfortable as I would liked.
mcQuestions.explanation: Comparative: 'more + adjective + than'; after 'would have', use past participle 'liked'.
mcQuestions.hint: Comparative: more + adjective + than; would have + past participle.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option correctly uses 'for all + noun phrase' as a concessive?
options: A misunderstanding, for all the awkwardness it creates, can sometimes cut through.
A misunderstanding, for all awkwardness it creates, can sometimes cut through.
A misunderstanding, for all of awkwardness it creates, can sometimes cut through.
A misunderstanding, for all the awkwardness it is creating, can sometimes cut through.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'For all + the + noun phrase' is a fixed concessive expression meaning 'despite all'; the noun takes the definite article.
mcQuestions.hint: Fixed phrase: 'for all the + noun' meaning 'despite all the'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Can a misunderstanding sometimes create a stronger bond than a smooth first meeting? What does this suggest about the role of vulnerability in building friendships?
